revision.text: [[File:Tamatsukuri Onsen - View from the bridge.jpg|thumb]]

[[Tamayu, Shimane]]
[[File:Tamatsukuri Onsen - View from the bridge.jpg|thumb]]

[[Tamayu, Shimane]]
[[File:Tamatsukuri Onsen - View from the bridge.jpg|thumb]]
[[fr:Tamatsukuri Onsen]] [[ja:玉造温泉]]

Tamatsukuri Onsen is located in [[Tamayu, Shimane]]. The closest large city is [[Matsue]], which can be accessed by car, bus, or rail.
Tamatsukuri Onsen is and ancient and renowned hot spring (onsen in Japanese) where the gods of Japan are said to bathe once a year since the creation of the archipelagic islands of Japan according to the myth of the gods [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]].
The water's of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism,skin conditions,etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and it's beauty benefits.
[[File:Tamatsukuri Onsen - View from the bridge.jpg|thumb]]
[[fr:Tamatsukuri Onsen]] [[ja:玉造温泉]]

[[File:Tamatsukuri Onsen - View from the bridge.jpg|thumb]]
[[fr:Tamatsukuri Onsen]] [[ja:玉造温泉]]

{{wikify|date=November 2011}} {{ref improve|date=November 2011}} [[File:Tamatsukuri onsen01st3200.jpg|300px|thumb|The '''[[Magatama]]''' bridge of Tamatsukuri Onsen]] {{nihongo|'''Tamatsukuri Onsen'''|玉造温泉}} is an [[onsen]] hot spring located in [[Tamayu, Shimane|Tamayu]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The closest large city to the onsen is [[Matsue]], which can be accessed by car, bus, or rail. Tamatsukuri is a noted [[sulfate]] spring with water temperatures between {{convert|50|°C|°F}} and {{convert|70|°C|°F}}.<ref name="d">{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 玉造温泉 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans_title = Tamatsukuri Onsen | oclc = 56431036}}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = S | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036}}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Tamatsukuri Hot Spring | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036}}</ref>
Tamatsukuri Onsen is an ancient and renowned hot spring (onsen in Japanese) where the gods of Japan are said to bathe once a year since the creation of the archipelagic islands of Japan according to the myth of the gods [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]]. The onsen is mentioned as early as 733 in an entry in the [[Izumo Fudoki]], an ancient record of the culture and geography of [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="d" />
The water's of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism, skin conditions, etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and its beauty benefits.
==Images==
[[File:Tamatsukuri Onsen - Cherry Blossom by the river1.jpg|thumb|left|380px|Cherry Blossom by the river]]
[[File:Tamatsukuri onsen04bs4592.jpg|thumb|center|400px|River going through Tamatsukuri Onsen]]
==References== {{Reflist|2}}
{{coord missing|Shimane Prefecture}}
[[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Geography of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Visitor attractions in Shimane Prefecture]]
[[fr:Tamatsukuri Onsen]] [[ja:玉造温泉]]

Tamatsukuri is a noted [[sulfate]] spring with water temperatures between {{convert|50|-|70|°C|°F}}.<ref name="d">{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 玉造温泉 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tamatsukuri Onsen | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = S | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Tamatsukuri Hot Spring | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref>
Tamatsukuri Onsen is an ancient [[hot spring]] where gods are said to bathe once a year since the creation of Japan according to the myth of [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]]. The onsen is mentioned as early as 733 in an entry in the Izumo Fudoki, an ancient record of the culture and geography of [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="d" />
The waters of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism, skin conditions, etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and its beauty benefits.

Tamatsukuri is a noted [[sulfate]] spring with water temperatures between {{convert|50|-|70|°C|°F}}.<ref name="d">{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 玉造温泉 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tamatsukuri Onsen | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = S | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Tamatsukuri Hot Spring | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref>
Tamatsukuri Onsen is an ancient [[hot spring]], dating back to the 8th century<ref name="jt">{{cite web |title=An ancient hot spring resort by a small river that flows into nearby Lake Shinji |url=https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/931/ |publisher=Japan Travel |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref> where gods are said to bathe once a year since the creation of Japan according to the myth of [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]]. The onsen is mentioned as early as 733 in an entry in the Izumo Fudoki, an ancient record of the culture and geography of [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="d" />
The waters of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism, skin conditions, etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and its beauty benefits.<ref name="SJ">{{cite web |title=Historical and modern hot spring resort, Tamatsukuri Onsen |date=15 May 2014 |url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/en/destination/2461 |publisher=Shimane Japan |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref>

Tamatsukuri Onsen is an ancient [[hot spring]], dating back to the 8th century<ref name="jt">{{cite web |title=An ancient hot spring resort by a small river that flows into nearby Lake Shinji |url=https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/931/ |publisher=Japan Travel |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref> where gods are said to bathe once a year since the creation of Japan according to the myth of [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]]. The onsen is mentioned as early as 733 in an entry in the Izumo Fudoki, an ancient record of the culture and geography of [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="d" />
The waters of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism, skin conditions, etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and its beauty benefits.<ref name="SJ">{{cite web |title=Historical and modern hot spring resort, Tamatsukuri Onsen |date=15 May 2014 |url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/en/destination/2461 |publisher=Shimane Japan |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref>

Tamatsukuri is a noted [[sulfate]] spring with water temperatures between {{convert|50|-|70|°C|°F}}.<ref name="d">{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 玉造温泉 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tamatsukuri Onsen | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = S | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Tamatsukuri Hot Spring | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref>
Tamatsukuri Onsen is an ancient [[hot spring]], dating back to the 8th century<ref name="jt">{{cite web |title=An ancient hot spring resort by a small river that flows into nearby Lake Shinji |url=https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/931/ |publisher=Japan Travel |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref> where gods are said to bathe once a year since the creation of Japan according to the myth of [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]]. The onsen is mentioned as early as 733 in an entry in the Izumo Fudoki, an ancient record of the culture and geography of [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="d" />
The waters of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism, skin conditions, etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and its beauty benefits.<ref name="SJ">{{cite web |title=Historical and modern hot spring resort, Tamatsukuri Onsen |date=15 May 2014 |url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/en/destination/2461 |publisher=Shimane Japan |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref>

{{short description|Hot spring in Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Tamatsukuri onsen01st3200.jpg|300px|thumb|The '''[[Magatama]]''' bridge of Tamatsukuri Onsen]] {{nihongo|'''Tamatsukuri Onsen'''|玉造温泉}} is an [[onsen]] hot spring located in [[Tamayu, Shimane|Tamayu]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The closest large city to the onsen is [[Matsue]], which can be accessed by car, bus, or rail. Tamatsukuri is a noted [[sulfate]] spring with water temperatures between {{convert|50|-|70|°C|°F}}.<ref name="d">{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 玉造温泉 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tamatsukuri Onsen | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = S | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Tamatsukuri Hot Spring | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref>
Tamatsukuri Onsen is an ancient [[hot spring]], dating back to the 8th century<ref name="jt">{{cite web |title=An ancient hot spring resort by a small river that flows into nearby Lake Shinji |url=https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/931/ |publisher=Japan Travel |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref> where gods are said to bathe once a year since the creation of Japan according to the myth of [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]]. The onsen is mentioned as early as 733 in an entry in the Izumo Fudoki, an ancient record of the culture and geography of [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="d" />
The waters of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism, skin conditions, etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and its beauty benefits.<ref name="SJ">{{cite web |title=Historical and modern hot spring resort, Tamatsukuri Onsen |date=15 May 2014 |url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/en/destination/2461 |publisher=Shimane Japan |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref>

The onsen is mentioned as early as 733 in an entry in the Izumo Fudoki, an ancient record of the culture and geography of [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="d" />
The waters of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism, skin conditions, etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and its beauty benefits.<ref name="SJ">{{cite web |title=Historical and modern hot spring resort, Tamatsukuri Onsen |date=15 May 2014 |url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/en/destination/2461 |publisher=Shimane Japan |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref>

The closest large city to the onsen is [[Matsue]], which can be accessed by car, bus, or rail. Tamatsukuri is a noted [[sulfate]] spring with water temperatures between {{convert|50|-|70|°C|°F}}.<ref name="d">{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 玉造温泉 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tamatsukuri Onsen | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = S | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Tamatsukuri Hot Spring | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref>

The onsen is mentioned as early as 733 in an entry in the Izumo Fudoki, an ancient record of the culture and geography of [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="d" />
The waters of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism, skin conditions, etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and its beauty benefits.<ref name="SJ">{{cite web |title=Historical and modern hot spring resort, Tamatsukuri Onsen |date=15 May 2014 |url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/en/destination/2461 |publisher=Shimane Japan |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref>

{{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Landforms of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Springs of Japan]] [[Category:Matsue, Shimane]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Sulfate Spring]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Onsen]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Onsen]]

{{short description|Hot spring in Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Tamatsukuri onsen01st3200.jpg|300px|thumb|The '''[[Magatama]]''' bridge of Tamatsukuri Onsen]] {{nihongo|'''Tamatsukuri Onsen'''|玉造温泉}} is an [[onsen]] hot spring located in [[Tamayu, Shimane|Tamayu]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The closest large city to the onsen is [[Matsue]], which can be accessed by car, bus, or rail. Tamatsukuri is a noted [[sulfate]] spring with water temperatures between {{convert|50|-|70|°C|°F}}.<ref name="d">{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 玉造温泉 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tamatsukuri Onsen | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = S | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Tamatsukuri Hot Spring | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref>
Tamatsukuri Onsen is an ancient [[hot spring]], dating back to the 8th century<ref name="jt">{{cite web |title=An ancient hot spring resort by a small river that flows into nearby Lake Shinji |url=https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/931/ |publisher=Japan Travel |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref> where gods are said to bathe once a year since the creation of Japan according to the myth of [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]]. The onsen is mentioned as early as 733 in an entry in the Izumo Fudoki, an ancient record of the culture and geography of [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="d" />
The waters of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism, skin conditions, etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and its beauty benefits.<ref name="SJ">{{cite web |title=Historical and modern hot spring resort, Tamatsukuri Onsen |date=15 May 2014 |url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/en/destination/2461 |publisher=Shimane Japan |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref>
==Images==

[[File:Tamatsukuri onsen04bs4592.jpg|thumb|center|400px|River going through Tamatsukuri Onsen]]

[[sn:Tamatsukuri Onsen]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Onsen]]

revision.comment: Bot: mark page as missing translated-page template
revision.text:
{{short description|Hot spring in Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Tamatsukuri onsen01st3200.jpg|300px|thumb|The '''[[Magatama]]''' bridge of Tamatsukuri Onsen]] {{nihongo|'''Tamatsukuri Onsen'''|玉造温泉}} is an [[onsen]] hot spring located in [[Tamayu, Shimane|Tamayu]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The closest large city to the onsen is [[Matsue]], which can be accessed by car, bus, or rail. Tamatsukuri is a noted [[sulfate]] spring with water temperatures between {{convert|50|-|70|°C|°F}}.<ref name="d">{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 玉造温泉 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tamatsukuri Onsen | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = S | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Tamatsukuri Hot Spring | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref>
Tamatsukuri Onsen is an ancient [[hot spring]], dating back to the 8th century<ref name="jt">{{cite web |title=An ancient hot spring resort by a small river that flows into nearby Lake Shinji |url=https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/931/ |publisher=Japan Travel |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref> where gods are said to bathe once a year since the creation of Japan according to the myth of [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]]. The onsen is mentioned as early as 733 in an entry in the Izumo Fudoki, an ancient record of the culture and geography of [[Izumo Province]].<ref name="d" />
The waters of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism, skin conditions, etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and its beauty benefits.<ref name="SJ">{{cite web |title=Historical and modern hot spring resort, Tamatsukuri Onsen |date=15 May 2014 |url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/en/destination/2461 |publisher=Shimane Japan |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref>
==Images==
[[File:Tamatsukuri Onsen - Cherry Blossom by the river1.jpg|thumb|left|380px|Cherry Blossom by the river]]
[[File:Tamatsukuri onsen04bs4592.jpg|thumb|center|400px|River going through Tamatsukuri Onsen]]
==References== {{Reflist|2}}

The onsen is mentioned as early as 733 in an entry in the Izumo Fudoki, an ancient record of the culture and geography of [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]].<ref name="d" />
The waters of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism, skin conditions, etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and its beauty benefits.<ref name="SJ">{{cite web |title=Historical and modern hot spring resort, Tamatsukuri Onsen |date=15 May 2014 |url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/en/destination/2461 |publisher=Shimane Japan |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref>
==Images==
[[File:Tamatsukuri Onsen - Cherry Blossom by the river1.jpg|thumb|left|380px|Cherry Blossom by the river]]
[[File:Tamatsukuri onsen04bs4592.jpg|thumb|center|400px|River going through Tamatsukuri Onsen]]

Tamatsukuri is a noted [[:en:sulfate|sulfate]] spring with water temperatures between {{convert|50|-|70|°C|°F}}.<ref name="d">{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 玉造温泉 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tamatsukuri Onsen | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = S | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Tamatsukuri Hot Spring | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref>
Tamatsukuri Onsen is an ancient [[:en:Hot spring|Hot spring]], dating back to the 8th century<ref name="jt">{{cite web |title=An ancient hot spring resort by a small river that flows into nearby Lake Shinji |url=https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/931/ |publisher=Japan Travel |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref> where gods are said to bathe once a year since the creation of Japan according to the myth of [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]. The onsen is mentioned as early as 733 in an entry in the Izumo Fudoki, an ancient record of the culture and geography of [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]].<ref name="d" />
The waters of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism, skin conditions, etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and its beauty benefits.<ref name="SJ">{{cite web |title=Historical and modern hot spring resort, Tamatsukuri Onsen |date=15 May 2014 |url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/en/destination/2461 |publisher=Shimane Japan |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref>

[[en:Tamatsukuri Onsen]]
[[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri Onsen}} [[fr:Tamatsukuri onsen]] [[id:Tamatsukuri Onsen]] [[ja:玉造温泉]] [[zh:玉造溫泉]] {{wikidata link|Q3514711}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[en:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[fr:Tamatsukuri onsen]][[id:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[ja:玉造温泉]][[zh:玉造溫泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3514711]] Wikidata name: Tamatsukuri Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Hot spring in Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Tamatsukuri onsen01st3200.jpg|300px|thumb|The '''[[Magatama]]''' bridge of Tamatsukuri Onsen]] {{nihongo|'''Tamatsukuri Onsen'''|玉造温泉}} is an [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] hot spring located in [[:en:Tamayu, Shimane|Tamayu]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. The closest large city to the onsen is [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], which can be accessed by car, bus, or rail. Tamatsukuri is a noted [[:en:sulfate|sulfate]] spring with water temperatures between {{convert|50|-|70|°C|°F}}.<ref name="d">{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 玉造温泉 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tamatsukuri Onsen | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = S | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Tamatsukuri Hot Spring | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref>
Tamatsukuri Onsen is an ancient [[:en:Hot spring|Hot spring]], dating back to the 8th century<ref name="jt">{{cite web |title=An ancient hot spring resort by a small river that flows into nearby Lake Shinji |url=https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/931/ |publisher=Japan Travel |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref> where gods are said to bathe once a year since the creation of Japan according to the myth of [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]. The onsen is mentioned as early as 733 in an entry in the Izumo Fudoki, an ancient record of the culture and geography of [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]].<ref name="d" />
The waters of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism, skin conditions, etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and its beauty benefits.<ref name="SJ">{{cite web |title=Historical and modern hot spring resort, Tamatsukuri Onsen |date=15 May 2014 |url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/en/destination/2461 |publisher=Shimane Japan |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref>
==Images==
[[File:Tamatsukuri Onsen - Cherry Blossom by the river1.jpg|thumb|left|380px|Cherry Blossom by the river]]
[[File:Tamatsukuri onsen04bs4592.jpg|thumb|center|400px|River going through Tamatsukuri Onsen]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[en:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[fr:Tamatsukuri onsen]][[id:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[ja:玉造温泉]][[zh:玉造溫泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[en:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[fr:Tamatsukuri onsen]][[id:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[ja:玉造温泉]][[zh:玉造溫泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[en:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[fr:Tamatsukuri onsen]][[id:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[ja:玉造温泉]][[zh:玉造溫泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3514711]] Wikidata name: Tamatsukuri Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Hot spring in Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Tamatsukuri onsen01st3200.jpg|300px|thumb|The '''[[Magatama]]''' bridge of Tamatsukuri Onsen]] {{nihongo|'''Tamatsukuri Onsen'''|玉造温泉}} is an [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] hot spring located in [[:en:Tamayu, Shimane|Tamayu]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. The closest large city to the onsen is [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], which can be accessed by car, bus, or rail. Tamatsukuri is a noted [[:en:sulfate|sulfate]] spring with water temperatures between {{convert|50|-|70|°C|°F}}.<ref name="d">{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 玉造温泉 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tamatsukuri Onsen | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = S | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Tamatsukuri Hot Spring | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref>
Tamatsukuri Onsen is an ancient [[:en:Hot spring|Hot spring]], dating back to the 8th century<ref name="jt">{{cite web |title=An ancient hot spring resort by a small river that flows into nearby Lake Shinji |url=https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/931/ |publisher=Japan Travel |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref> where gods are said to bathe once a year since the creation of Japan according to the myth of [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]. The onsen is mentioned as early as 733 in an entry in the Izumo Fudoki, an ancient record of the culture and geography of [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]].<ref name="d" />
The waters of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism, skin conditions, etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and its beauty benefits.<ref name="SJ">{{cite web |title=Historical and modern hot spring resort, Tamatsukuri Onsen |date=15 May 2014 |url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/en/destination/2461 |publisher=Shimane Japan |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref>
==Images==
[[File:Tamatsukuri Onsen - Cherry Blossom by the river1.jpg|thumb|left|380px|Cherry Blossom by the river]]
[[File:Tamatsukuri onsen04bs4592.jpg|thumb|center|400px|River going through Tamatsukuri Onsen]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[en:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[fr:Tamatsukuri onsen]][[id:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[ja:玉造温泉]][[zh:玉造溫泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[en:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[fr:Tamatsukuri onsen]][[id:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[ja:玉造温泉]][[zh:玉造溫泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[en:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[fr:Tamatsukuri onsen]][[id:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[ja:玉造温泉]][[zh:玉造溫泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3514711]] Wikidata name: Tamatsukuri Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Hot spring in Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Tamatsukuri onsen01st3200.jpg|300px|thumb|The '''[[Magatama]]''' bridge of Tamatsukuri Onsen]] {{nihongo|'''Tamatsukuri Onsen'''|玉造温泉}} is an {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} hot spring located in {{ill|Tamayu, Shimane|en|Tamayu, Shimane|WD=Q3514716|lt=Tamayu}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}. The closest large city to the onsen is {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue}}, which can be accessed by car, bus, or rail. Tamatsukuri is a noted {{ill|sulfate|en|sulfate|WD=Q25997675|lt=sulfate}} spring with water temperatures between {{convert|50|-|70|°C|°F}}.<ref name="d">{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 玉造温泉 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tamatsukuri Onsen | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = S | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Tamatsukuri Hot Spring | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref>
Tamatsukuri Onsen is an ancient {{ill|Hot spring|en|Hot spring|WD=Q177380|lt=Hot spring}}, dating back to the 8th century<ref name="jt">{{cite web |title=An ancient hot spring resort by a small river that flows into nearby Lake Shinji |url=https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/931/ |publisher=Japan Travel |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref> where gods are said to bathe once a year since the creation of Japan according to the myth of [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}}. The onsen is mentioned as early as 733 in an entry in the Izumo Fudoki, an ancient record of the culture and geography of {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|WD=Q582846|lt=Izumo Province}}.<ref name="d" />
The waters of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism, skin conditions, etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and its beauty benefits.<ref name="SJ">{{cite web |title=Historical and modern hot spring resort, Tamatsukuri Onsen |date=15 May 2014 |url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/en/destination/2461 |publisher=Shimane Japan |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref>
==Images==
[[File:Tamatsukuri Onsen - Cherry Blossom by the river1.jpg|thumb|left|380px|Cherry Blossom by the river]]
[[File:Tamatsukuri onsen04bs4592.jpg|thumb|center|400px|River going through Tamatsukuri Onsen]]
==References== {{Reflist|2}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[en:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[fr:Tamatsukuri onsen]][[id:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[ja:玉造温泉]][[zh:玉造溫泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[en:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[fr:Tamatsukuri onsen]][[id:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[ja:玉造温泉]][[zh:玉造溫泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[en:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[fr:Tamatsukuri onsen]][[id:Tamatsukuri Onsen]][[ja:玉造温泉]][[zh:玉造溫泉]]
{{short description|Hot spring in Shimane Prefecture, Japan}} [[File:Tamatsukuri onsen01st3200.jpg|300px|thumb|The '''[[Magatama]]''' bridge of Tamatsukuri Onsen]] {{nihongo|'''Tamatsukuri Onsen'''|玉造温泉}} is an {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} hot spring located in {{ill|Tamayu, Shimane|en|Tamayu, Shimane|WD=Q3514716|lt=Tamayu}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}. The closest large city to the onsen is {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue}}, which can be accessed by car, bus, or rail. Tamatsukuri is a noted {{ill|sulfate|en|sulfate|WD=Q25997675|lt=sulfate}} spring with water temperatures between {{convert|50|-|70|°C|°F}}.<ref name="d">{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 玉造温泉 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tamatsukuri Onsen | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = S | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Tamatsukuri Hot Spring | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-10-23 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}</ref>
Tamatsukuri Onsen is an ancient {{ill|Hot spring|en|Hot spring|WD=Q177380|lt=Hot spring}}, dating back to the 8th century<ref name="jt">{{cite web |title=An ancient hot spring resort by a small river that flows into nearby Lake Shinji |url=https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/931/ |publisher=Japan Travel |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref> where gods are said to bathe once a year since the creation of Japan according to the myth of [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}}. The onsen is mentioned as early as 733 in an entry in the Izumo Fudoki, an ancient record of the culture and geography of {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|WD=Q582846|lt=Izumo Province}}.<ref name="d" />
The waters of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism, skin conditions, etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and its beauty benefits.<ref name="SJ">{{cite web |title=Historical and modern hot spring resort, Tamatsukuri Onsen |date=15 May 2014 |url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/en/destination/2461 |publisher=Shimane Japan |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref>

Tamatsukuri Onsen is an ancient {{ill|Hot spring|en|Hot spring|WD=Q177380|lt=Hot spring|1=Hot spring}}, dating back to the 8th century<ref name="jt">{{cite web |title=An ancient hot spring resort by a small river that flows into nearby Lake Shinji |url=https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/931/ |publisher=Japan Travel |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref> where gods are said to bathe once a year since the creation of Japan according to the myth of [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}}. The onsen is mentioned as early as 733 in an entry in the Izumo Fudoki, an ancient record of the culture and geography of {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|WD=Q582846|lt=Izumo Province|1=Izumo Province}}.<ref name="d" />
The waters of Tamatsukuri are said to aid in the recovery from several ailments such as rheumatism, skin conditions, etc. The mineral content of the water is said to be beneficial to the skin, and is a popular destination with those interested in the area's ancient history, and its beauty benefits.<ref name="SJ">{{cite web |title=Historical and modern hot spring resort, Tamatsukuri Onsen |date=15 May 2014 |url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/en/destination/2461 |publisher=Shimane Japan |access-date=14 May 2021}}</ref>
